response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine staff-level pedigree, solid distributed-systems history (Sociomantic's millions-of-requests-per-second microservices stack), and current hands-on work with agentic tooling and MCP frameworks at re:cinq — exactly the AI-native exposure the role values. However, the single hardest must-have gap is Python: it does not appear anywhere in his resume, skills list, or project descriptions, while the role lists it as a core requirement. His AI-native experience is also very recent (two months), and stakeholder or cross-functional leadership evidence is thin. Worth a screening call to probe the Python situation and depth of AI-systems work before committing to a full loop.
strengths: Current role at re:cinq directly involves agentic workflows and MCP code generators — demonstrates comfort in AI-native engineering environments ahead of most candidates
Proven distributed-systems experience at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, async I/O architecture in a production microservices environment
Consistent staff and senior IC trajectory with evidence of technical leadership, community teaching (workshops, conference talks), and cross-stack ownership across multiple companies
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the resume, skills list, and all project descriptions — this is a stated must-have and the gap is unambiguous
Hands-on AI/agentic experience began only in April 2025 (~2 months at time of resume), making it difficult to assess depth in LLM infrastructure or AI-driven development practices

No clear evidence of cross-functional stakeholder management, product-bridge responsibilities, or operating in agile ceremonies at the scale implied by the role (rotating scrum champion, leadership coordination)
